content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 2, 2011
Sharon Levin
475 Quartz Street
Redwood City, CA 94062

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our file no. A-11-095

Dear Ms. Levin:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the honorarium provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Is the compensation you receive for giving lectures about children's book outside the state reportable under the Act?

CONCLUSION

No. According to your conflict of interest code, you are only required to report income or gifts received from a source within your jurisdiction that has business relationships within Redwood City, including any person or business entity that requests funds or other consideration from the Redwood City Library Board. Because the compensation you received from a speech you gave in Nevada was from a Nevada entity, it does not appear to be from a source that you are required to report on your Form 700.

FACTS

You are a former Library Board member in Redwood City. Board members serve on a volunteer-basis and are appointed by the Redwood City Council. You resigned from that Board in January of 2011. For that position, you filed a Statement of Economic Interests ("Form 700") based on your disclosure category in the Library Board's conflict of interest code. You filed your 2010 form before April 1, 2011, as required. You asked at the time whether your work as a children's book reviewer and speaker would be impacted by your position on the Library Board.

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-11-095
Page No. 2

You were told that it would not. You filed your form based on that information. You have
stated you will amend your form if necessary.

You also write reviews for children's books and occasionally give talks about these
books to various groups. You are paid a small stipend for the reviews and the talks. While you
do claim this income on your taxes, you have not previously filed taxes with these activities as a
"business," though you intend to do so in the future. You have records for previous years' work.
In 2010, you gave one speech for which you received compensation. The speech was in Nevada
for "Nevada Reading Week."

Based on the Library Board's disclosure categories for its conflict of interest code, you
are required to report any income received from "business relationships within Redwood City,"
including any person or business entity that requests funds or other consideration from the
Redwood City Library Board. "Income" under the Act includes gifts. (See Section 82030(a).)

ANALYSIS

You are only required to report income or gifts received from a source within your
jurisdiction of Redwood City that have "business relationships within Redwood City," including
any person or business entity that requests funds or other consideration from the Redwood City
Library Board. Because the compensation you received from a speech you gave in Nevada was
from a Nevada entity, it does not appear to be from a source that you are required to report on
your Form 700. Therefore, you do not need to amend your Form 700 to reflect income you
received from work performed in Nevada. You therefore do not need to amend your Statement of
Economic Interests to reflect income you received from Nevada.

For future reference, Section 89502(c) provides that "no designated employee of a state
or local government agency shall accept an honorarium from any source if the member or
employee would be required to report the receipt of income or gifts from that source on his or her
statement of economic interests." Thus, if it is not reportable on your form 700, it is not a
prohibited honorarium under the Act.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

HMR:jgl
